skill.name: skill-check
skill.description: Tests and validates Agent Skills through static checks, script tests, eval regression, security review, and report generation.
skill.summary: Validate Agent Skills for quality, security, structure, and release readiness.
skill.author: JckJhns

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many dangerous patterns, but review shows most are intentional security-review examples or test fixtures. The skill is not blocked because no malicious intent or prompt injection was found, but Standard and Deep checks can execute untrusted skill scripts and require user review before running.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Skill Scripts May Be Executed During Deeper Checks
medium_findings.description: Standard and Deep workflows include script execution with mock inputs after a security review. This is a legitimate testing feature, but running scripts from an arbitrary skill can execute attacker-controlled code if the preliminary review misses a risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly includes script execution. The skill also instructs reviewers to warn before executing scripts with critical findings, which reduces but does not remove the risk.

low_findings.title: Dangerous Patterns Are Primarily Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Many external command, eval, sensitive path, and credential patterns appear in security-review guidance that teaches the agent what to flag. These are not instructions to perform malicious actions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited section is under a security review heading and defines which unsafe patterns should be reported. The surrounding context confirms educational intent.

low_findings.title: Intentionally Insecure Fixture Contains Real Anti-Patterns
low_findings.description: The insecure fixture includes a fake API key, disabled TLS verification, os.system, eval, and a sensitive path write. The file states it is a test fixture and should not be used in production.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky code is real, but the header explicitly marks it as a deliberate test fixture. Its use in eval expectations supports that interpretation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Execution Pattern In Fixture
dangerous_patterns.description: The insecure fixture uses os.system with a user command. This is dangerous if copied into real skills, but it is included as a detection target.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly visible and unsafe in normal code. The fixture context makes it non-malicious in this package.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Code Evaluation Pattern In Fixture
dangerous_patterns.description: The insecure fixture evaluates a supplied expression. This is arbitrary code execution in normal code and should only appear in controlled test fixtures.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The eval call is direct and clearly risky. The file header and eval description show it is intentional test data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Path And Network Patterns In Fixture
dangerous_patterns.description: The insecure fixture downloads arbitrary URLs with TLS verification disabled and writes to a system path. These are warning-level risks outside test data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network and file-write patterns are explicit. The fixture label reduces publication risk but does not make the pattern safe to reuse.

content.value_statement: Agent Skills can fail through broken references, weak evals, unsafe scripts, or unclear instructions. SkillCheck gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured process for testing quality, security, and readiness.
actual_capabilities: Discovers skill files and builds a manifest before testing.
Runs Quick, Standard, or Deep validation workflows based on requested depth.
Checks references, scripts, evals, I/O contracts, and best practices.
Reviews scripts and instructions for security risks before execution.
Produces structured reports with verdicts, evidence, and prioritized fixes.
limitations: Standard and Deep checks may execute untrusted scripts after review.
Some mock file generation depends on optional third-party Python packages.
End-to-end tests can be influenced by shared context on platforms without subagents.
Security results are advisory and still require human review for publication decisions.

use_cases.title: Pre-Publish Skill Review
use_cases.description: Audit a skill directory before sharing it with users or submitting it to a marketplace.

use_cases.title: Regression Testing For Skill Updates
use_cases.description: Run evals and script checks after editing a skill to catch broken behavior early.

use_cases.title: Security Triage For Community Skills
use_cases.description: Inspect downloaded skills for risky scripts, dangerous instructions, and unsafe file access.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Safety Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one skill directory.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Quick Check on this skill directory and report security, structure, and best practice issues.

prompt_templates.title: Standard Release Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Standard Check on this skill. Include eval regression, script tests with mock inputs, and I/O contract validation.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate A Failure
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused debugging after a failed skill test.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the failed SkillCheck results and identify the root cause, affected files, and the smallest safe fix.

prompt_templates.title: Deep Marketplace Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Deep Check on this skill for marketplace readiness. Include edge cases, end-to-end workflow tests, security review, and prioritized remediation.

output_examples.input: Run a Quick Check on a small documentation skill.
output_examples.output: A concise report with a verdict, passed and failed checks, missing references, security notes, and three prioritized fixes.

output_examples.input: Run a Standard Check on a skill with scripts and evals.
output_examples.output: A release-readiness report covering eval results, script execution, generated mock inputs, output validation, and any safety warnings.
A progress report that lists completed parts, missing files, placeholders, unclear outputs, and next steps instead of a failure-only verdict.

best_practices: Start with Quick Check when the skill source is unfamiliar or untrusted.
Review any Critical security findings before running Standard or Deep checks.
Use the report evidence to fix root causes before rerunning the same depth.
anti_patterns: Do not execute scripts from an unknown skill before reading the security findings.
Do not treat best practice scores as the same thing as functional pass rates.
Do not publish a skill only because one happy-path test passed.

faq.question: What does SkillCheck test?
faq.answer: It tests skill structure, references, scripts, evals, input and output contracts, security risks, and best practices.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes Agent Skills workflows for Claude, Claude Code, Codex, and compatible platforms.

faq.question: Can it run scripts?
faq.answer: Yes, Standard and Deep checks can run scripts with mock inputs after a security review.

faq.question: Is it safe to use on community skills?
faq.answer: Use Quick Check first. Review security findings before allowing script execution from community sources.

faq.question: What is the difference between Quick, Standard, and Deep?
faq.answer: Quick is static review, Standard adds eval and script testing, and Deep adds edge cases and end-to-end workflows.

faq.question: Does it create or edit skills?
faq.answer: No. It reviews existing skills and reports problems, but it does not create new skills.
